questions.text: What does 'Al-Ghashiyah' mean?
questions.answer: The Overwhelming (another name for the Day of Judgment).

questions.text: How will the faces of the disbelievers look on that Day?
Describe the state of the disbelievers' work in the world (Verse 3).
What will the disbelievers enter?
What will they be given to drink in Hell?
What is 'Dari'?
questions.answer: A bitter, thorny plant in Hell which is the only food for the sinners.

questions.text: Does 'Dari' nourish or satisfy hunger?
questions.answer: No, 'it neither nourishes nor avails against hunger'.

questions.text: How will the faces of the believers look on that Day?
Are the believers satisfied with their effort (Verse 9)?
Where will the believers reside?
What will the believers NOT hear in Paradise?
List three physical rewards in Paradise mentioned in Verses 12-16.
questions.answer: A flowing spring, raised couches, and cushions set in rows.

questions.text: What animal does Allah ask man to look at to observe His creation?
What three other parts of nature are mentioned as signs of creation in Verses 18-20?
questions.answer: The sky (how it is raised), the mountains (how they are fixed), and the earth (how it is spread).

questions.text: What is the Prophet's (SAW) primary job according to Verse 21?
Is the Prophet a 'dictator' or 'manager' over the people?
questions.answer: No, 'you are not over them a controller (Musaytir)'.

questions.text: Who will Allah punish with the 'greatest punishment'?
questions.answer: The one who turns away and disbelieves.

questions.text: Where is the final return of all people?
Whose responsibility is the 'account' (judgment) of the people?
What is the central contrast in this Surah?
questions.answer: The contrast between the extreme suffering and humiliation of the disbelievers vs the extreme luxury and joy of the believers.
